verses.text: Though I speak with tongues and have not charity, 'I am become as sounding brass'; it 'profiteth me nothing.' Love essential. Without love, the greatest gifts are empty.
Though I speak with tongues and have not charity, 'I am become as sounding brass'; it 'profiteth me nothing.' Love essential. Without love, the greatest gifts are empty.
Though I speak with tongues and have not charity, 'I am become as sounding brass'; it 'profiteth me nothing.' Love essential. Without love, the greatest gifts are empty.
'Charity suffereth long, and is kind'; envieth not, is not puffed up, 'seeketh not her own.' Love's character. True love is patient, humble, and selfless.
'Charity suffereth long, and is kind'; envieth not, is not puffed up, 'seeketh not her own.' Love's character. True love is patient, humble, and selfless.
It rejoices in the truth; 'beareth all things, believeth all things, hopeth all things, endureth all things.' Love's endurance. It bears and hopes without ceasing.
It rejoices in the truth; 'beareth all things, believeth all things, hopeth all things, endureth all things.' Love's endurance. It bears and hopes without ceasing.
'Charity never faileth'; prophecies and tongues shall fail; 'we know in part.' Love abides. Gifts pass, but love endures for ever.
'Charity never faileth'; prophecies and tongues shall fail; 'we know in part.' Love abides. Gifts pass, but love endures for ever.
'Charity never faileth'; prophecies and tongues shall fail; 'we know in part.' Love abides. Gifts pass, but love endures for ever.
I put away childish things; 'now we see through a glass, darkly; but then face to face.' Fuller sight. Now we know in part; then we shall fully know.
I put away childish things; 'now we see through a glass, darkly; but then face to face.' Fuller sight. Now we know in part; then we shall fully know.
'Now abideth faith, hope, charity, these three; but the greatest of these is charity.' Love supreme. Of the three abiding graces, love is the greatest.
